gt_violations.action: Couvert par STU-534. La cause racine (le détecteur d'alias par pattern) est supprimée par STU-538, en review — re-valider ce point après son merge.

gt_violations.signal: INFOBOX_ALIAS_CROSS [Mr Beaver->Mrs Beaver]: infobox 'alias=Beavers, Mr Beaver, Mrs Beaver'
gt_violations.first_seen_in: entities_classified.json (alias_resolution, method=title_alias, confidence=medium, merged_from=['Beavers'], evidence snippet 'Mrs Beaver / Beavers')
gt_violations.action: Ne pas fusionner deux entités dont les titres honorifiques diffèrent (Mr/Mrs) sur un nom de famille commun. Distinct de STU-534 (method=pattern) et NON couvert par STU-538, qui ne retire que le détecteur pattern.

gt_note: Corpus ground-truth Narnia créé le 2026-07-16 sous library/c_w_lewis/narnia/books/ground-truth/ (8 fichiers, 14 entités : Aslan, les quatre enfants, la Sorcière Blanche, Tumnus, les Beavers, Maugrim, Narnia + Cair Paravel, le Professeur + Mrs Macready). Étapes 1b/1c rejouées sur le wiki_pages.json de cette run : 3 violations brutes / 2 distinctes, toutes de classe alias-identité, toutes en amont dans entities_classified.json. Zéro signal d'hallucination, zéro terme interdit, zéro relation future — la génération sur les 9 pages abouties est propre au regard du GT. Chaque fait du corpus est vérifié par grep contre chapters.json, ce qui a corrigé deux erreurs de mémoire : 'Jadis' EST dans LWW (1 occurrence, dans l'avis d'arrestation signé Maugrim) et 'Pevensie' n'y est PAS (0 occurrence) — le patronyme est donc un signal d'hallucination, pas un fait.
gt_regression_detail: ⚠️ partiel : 0 signal d'hallucination, 0 terme interdit, 0 relation future sur les 9 pages abouties ; 2 contaminations d'identité par alias (Lucy←Peter, Beaver←Mrs Beaver), toutes deux introduites en amont par alias-resolution, aucune imputable au rédacteur LLM.

root_causes_verified.cause: studio run --json stdout truncated at exactly 8192 bytes (nondeterministic Node stdout flush race); extract_first_json_object then silently returns the nested stages[0] fragment, so run_id becomes the stage id and the JSONL fallback misses too
root_causes_verified.evidence: raw_response byte length == 8192 on all 3 saved artifacts; load_studio_stage_output('d9f310e4') recovers 'Cair Paravel', load_studio_stage_output('6fea0d4f') returns None
root_causes_verified.action: Fail on truncated top-level JSON instead of returning a fragment; read run_id independently and always prefer the complete .studio/runs JSONL log

root_causes_verified.signal: Peter Pevensie absent from the wiki; Lucy carries alias 'Peter'; Aslan carries alias 'Queen'
root_causes_verified.cause: 7 of 9 en.json alias_pattern_templates (6 of 7 in fr.json) reference only {b} — {a} never enters the regex, so any 'called her X' sentence merges X into an unconstrained partner
root_causes_verified.evidence: template '\bcalled (?:him|her|them) {b}\b' matched a='Peter' b='Queen Lucy' on a sentence that never mentions Peter; reproduced at HEAD via detect_named_aliases
root_causes_verified.action: Anchor {a} in every template; add a negative test where the snippet names only b

root_causes_verified.signal: 30 PERSON / 1 PLACE; Narnia (the world) has a character page
root_causes_verified.cause: Book takes the default spacy backend; en_core_web_lg tags Narnia PERSON 7/7 and Cair Paravel ORG on the real chapter text — upstream model limit on an invented world, not a routing defect
root_causes_verified.evidence: spaCy en_core_web_lg run directly on 3 chapters: PERSON 178, GPE 1, LOC 1
root_causes_verified.action: Evaluate and flip to backend: gliner (STU-521)

stale_artifacts: chapters.json was extracted at 18:00, before STU-529 (section filter) merged at 19:44 — section_filter.json is absent and the publisher blurb ('Peter, Susan, Edmund, and Lucy find their way through an old wardrobe...') is chapter 'annotation' in the corpus, feeding relationship sample_contexts. Re-extract before treating this as a live defect.
verdict: Two live bugs, both proven by replay: every one of the 19 'failed' pages was successfully generated and then discarded by an 8192-byte stdout truncation the recovery path was one id away from surviving (STU-533); and an alias rule whose regex cannot see one of its two operands merged Peter Pevensie into Lucy at confidence:high (STU-534). Generation quality on the 9 surviving pages is otherwise clean — 0 prefixed keys, 0 EPUB ids, 0 hallucinations, 100% FR.
new_issues: STU-533: studio run --json stdout truncates at 8192 bytes → 19 fully-generated pages discarded (bug)
STU-534: 7 of 9 alias_pattern_templates never anchor {a} → Peter merged into Lucy (bug)
STU-535: Narnia 01 on the spaCy NER backend: 30 PERSON / 1 PLACE (config/eval)

STU-541: alias-resolution method=title_alias merges Mr Beaver + Mrs Beaver into one 'Beaver' entity — honorifics treated as role_words; distinct from STU-534 (method=pattern) and not covered by STU-538 (bug)

gt_violations.signal: INFOBOX_ALIAS_CROSS [Peter->Lucy]: alias='LUCY, Lucy, Queen Lucy' — Lucy has no page
gt_violations.first_seen_in: library/c_w_lewis/narnia/registry.json (series registry, decision strategy='pattern' — a detector deleted by STU-538), replayed into splits.json via Registry.load_seed_table
gt_violations.action: Refuse a seed union whose backing decision names a retired strategy; purge the poisoned Narnia decisions. STU-534/538 fixed the producer and never invalidated the artifact.

gt_violations.signal: INFOBOX_ALIAS_CROSS [Mr Beaver->Mrs Beaver]: alias='Mr Beaver, Mrs Beaver'
gt_violations.first_seen_in: splits.json (entity-clustering, cluster_006 — reproduced cold at HEAD, no seed needed)
gt_violations.action: Port STU-541's honorific-conflict refusal into the clustering matcher; STU-541 only patched alias_resolution, one stage too late.

gt_violations.first_seen_in: llm_generation — absent from entities_classified, relationships, chapter_summaries and every batch
gt_violations.action: Patronyme exact au regard du canon (Lewis le donne a partir de Prince Caspian) mais absent de l'EPUB du tome 1 : 0 occurrence, verifie sur le texte brut. Fait vrai, non sourcable — signal de memoire, pas d'erreur factuelle. Severite basse, pas d'issue ; le signal GT reste correct et doit rester en place.
LWW does say the Emperor put the Deep Magic 'into Narnia at the very beginning' — the page renders that, it does not import The Magician's Nephew. Tighten the forbidden phrase.

absent_characters: Lucy (merged into Peter by the poisoned seed — STU-584)

weak_entities: Sons of Adam / Fauns / Daughters of Eve / Humans / Centaurs (FACTION: total_mentions=0, empty stub pages at principal tier — STU-586)
Robin (typed PLACE — it is Robin redbreast, a bird)
Son of Adam / Daughter of Eve / Prince / Emperor (bare titles promoted to PERSON pages)
gt_regression_detail: 4 violations / 3 real. Two identity contaminations survive from run 1 (Peter←Lucy, Beaver←Mrs Beaver) — both fixes shipped and neither reached this run: one is replayed by the series registry seed, the other lives in a stage the fix never touched. One LLM hallucination ('Lucy Pevensie', surname absent from LWW). 'la création de Narnia' is a probable GT false positive.

root_causes_verified.signal: 0/64 relations typed, 4/25 pages render a Relations section
root_causes_verified.cause: fold_relationships hardcodes relationship_type/direction/evolution to None (written for the untyped STU-435 co-occurrence graph); STU-556 routed the already-typed relationships_discovered.json through the same fold, and _merge_classification(pre_typed=True) only contributes prose keys, so nothing restores the erased type
root_causes_verified.first_seen_in: relationships_classified.json (types present in relationships_discovered.json one stage earlier)
root_causes_verified.evidence: fold_relationships(discovered, registry): in typed 32/32 -> out typed 0/32, reproduced on the shipped artifact
root_causes_verified.action: Preserve the type of a pre-typed edge, or skip the fold on a pre-typed graph (it merges nothing there, it only nulls)

root_causes_verified.signal: Lucy has no page; Peter carries her aliases and 336 pooled mentions
root_causes_verified.cause: series registry holds a decision with strategy='pattern' (detector deleted by STU-538) merging lucy/peter; Registry.accumulate carries it forward and entity_clustering seeds from it, re-applying the merge upstream of every rule that was fixed
root_causes_verified.evidence: at HEAD should_cluster_tokens('King Peter','Queen Lucy') and should_cluster_jw are both False, and alias_resolution proposes nothing — the pair merges only when seeded
root_causes_verified.action: Invalidate seed unions backed by a retired strategy; a decision is a cached verdict and must be keyed on the code that produced it

root_causes_verified.signal: Mr Beaver + Mrs Beaver still one entity after STU-541
root_causes_verified.cause: the merge is in entity-clustering, not alias-resolution: subset match strips nothing ({beaver} subset of {mr,beaver}) and jaro-winkler reads 'mr beaver'/'mrs beaver' as an orthographic variant
root_causes_verified.evidence: should_cluster_tokens('Mr Beaver','Beaver')=True, should_cluster_jw('Mr Beaver','Mrs Beaver')=True at HEAD, cold
root_causes_verified.action: Port the honorific-conflict refusal into clustering, per entity not per name (STU-541's transitivity trap)

root_causes_verified.signal: 5 empty principal pages (Sons of Adam, Fauns, Daughters of Eve, Humans, Centaurs)
root_causes_verified.cause: get_total_mentions' type_to_registry names only PERSON/PLACE/ORG/EVENT; FACTION has been first-order since STU-505 and factions_full.json is never read, so every FACTION lands at total_mentions=0 with an empty bundle, then the sub-threshold percentile fallback promotes it to principal
root_causes_verified.first_seen_in: entities_classified.json (total_mentions=0 while factions_full.json has mention_count=3 for Humans)
root_causes_verified.evidence: factions_full.json entity_021 Humans mention_count 3 across 2 chapters vs entities_classified Humans total_mentions 0
root_causes_verified.action: Derive the registry map from entity_taxonomy export.full_key instead of a fifth hardcoded table

resolved_since_run_1: STU-533 (8192-byte stdout truncation): 0 _failed pages this run, down from 17 — fix holds
STU-535 (spaCy NER on an invented world): the book is on GLiNER now; Narnia and Cair Paravel are PLACE, the Stone Table exists, Father Christmas / Maugrim / Giant Rumblebuffin arrive
verdict: The two run-1 blockers are gone (0 failed pages, correct PLACE typing) and a third has taken their place: fold_relationships nulls the relation types the new schema-guided discovery produces, so a graph that is 100% typed at discovery reaches the reader 0% typed and 21 of 25 pages render no relations at all. Both run-1 alias merges also survive, each because the fix landed in a different place than the bug: Peter/Lucy is replayed by a series-registry decision whose producing code was deleted, Mr/Mrs Beaver is decided in clustering, a stage STU-541 never touched.
new_issues: STU-583: fold_relationships wipes the discovered relation types — 0/32 typed pairs reach the pages (bug, urgent)
STU-584: the series registry replays a deleted alias strategy — Lucy still merged into Peter after STU-534/538 (bug, urgent)
STU-585: entity-clustering remarries Mr Beaver and Mrs Beaver — STU-541's rule lives only in alias-resolution (bug)
STU-586: FACTION entities count 0 mentions — 5 empty principal pages (bug)
